CCR.Cause: Confirmed FC-006 fault. Inspected hose connection, hose condition, patient-end hose sensor area, and elbow sensor path. Fault appeared related to hose assembly, elbow sensor path, or internal sensor circuit depending on substitution testing.
CCR.Resolution: Removed unit from service, reseated and inspected hose, checked for damage or contamination, tested with known-good hose if available, replaced faulty hose or serviced elbow sensor path as indicated, completed calibration/functional verification, and returned to service only after FC-006 cleared.

title: 3M Bair Hugger 775 Series Patient Warming System - Error: FC-007 Hose End Sensor 2 Open / Hose Connection Fault
description: Troubleshooting guide for Error FC-007 on the 3M Bair Hugger 775 Series Patient Warming System, indicating hose end sensor 2 open, possible hose disconnection, faulty hose, connector issue, elbow sensor problem, or related sensing fault.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the Bair Hugger 775 from patient use if Error FC-007 is active, recurring, or preventing normal warming operation. If warming therapy is still needed, have clinical staff transfer the patient to another approved warming unit. Do not continue using the unit if hose temperature sensing is unreliable.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power the unit on and confirm Error FC-007 is present. Ask staff whether the error occurred at startup, after connecting the hose, during use, or intermittently when the hose was moved. Check whether the unit runs, alarms, shuts down, or prevents warming operation.

steps.title: Check Whether the Hose Is Disconnected
steps.instructions: Inspect the hose connection at the warming unit. Confirm the hose is fully seated and is not loose, partially disconnected, crooked, or pulled away from the unit. Remove and reattach the hose securely, then power cycle the unit if needed and check whether FC-007 clears.

steps.title: Inspect the Hose and Connector Area
steps.instructions: Inspect the hose connector, contacts, elbow area, and hose body for damage, bent contacts, loose fit, cracks, pinching, liquid intrusion, debris, contamination, or rough handling damage. Confirm the connection area is clean, intact, and properly seated.

steps.title: Try a Known-Good Hose, If Available
steps.instructions: Remove the suspect hose and install a known-good compatible hose. Power on the warming unit and check whether Error FC-007 clears. If the error clears with the known-good hose, the original hose or hose sensor assembly is the likely cause.

steps.title: Check for Elbow Sensor Issue
steps.instructions: Inspect the elbow sensor area for visible damage, poor seating, loose fit, or signs of failure. Do not perform deep internal disassembly during routine floor troubleshooting. If the elbow sensor is a replaceable service part in your facility workflow, replace it as appropriate and complete required calibration and functional testing.

steps.title: Calibrate and Verify Before Return to Service
steps.instructions: Perform required calibration after replacing the hose or elbow sensor as applicable. Run the unit through normal operating modes and confirm Error FC-007 does not return, the hose is secure, airflow is present, temperature operation appears normal, alarms and indicators behave normally, and no unusual heat, smell, noise, or shutdown occurs.

CCR.Complaint: 3M Bair Hugger 775 displayed Error FC-007 during startup or use. Staff reported hose end sensor 2 open message and possible hose connection issue.
CCR.Cause: Hose connection was found loose at the warming unit, causing an open hose sensor condition. Error cleared after the hose was reseated.
CCR.Resolution: Reattached hose securely to warming unit. Powered unit on and verified Error FC-007 cleared. Ran functional check, confirmed normal operation, and returned unit to service.

title: 3M Bair Hugger 775 Series Patient Warming System - Error: FC-050 Stuck key during startup
description: Troubleshooting Error FC-050 on the 3M Bair Hugger 775 Series when a stuck key during startup prevents normal startup or reliable user interface operation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the warmer from patient use if Error FC-050 appears during startup or before use. Provide another warming unit if patient warming is needed and do not use the affected unit on a patient until the issue is resolved.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power the unit on and confirm Error FC-050 appears during startup. Note whether the error is constant or intermittent and document any keypad or control panel symptoms.

steps.title: Check for Obvious External Causes
steps.instructions: Inspect the control panel for physical damage, contamination, cleaning residue, tape residue, or an object pressing against the keypad during startup.

steps.title: Clean and Recheck the User Interface Surface
steps.instructions: Power the unit off, unplug it, clean the control panel surface using approved methods, allow it to dry completely, and restart the unit to see whether FC-050 returns.

steps.title: Check for Physical Keypad Response
steps.instructions: With the unit powered off, gently press each key and check whether any button feels stuck, collapsed, unusually firm, unusually soft, or does not rebound normally.

steps.title: Power Cycle and Recheck
steps.instructions: Turn the unit off, unplug the power cord, wait briefly, reconnect to a known good outlet, and observe whether the unit completes startup without Error FC-050.

steps.title: Isolate the Fault
steps.instructions: If FC-050 returns after external checks, cleaning, and restart, remove the unit from service and treat the issue as a device-related user interface fault.

steps.title: Repair Action
steps.instructions: Replace the defective user interface per approved service procedure. Perform required calibration and operational verification before returning the unit to service.

CCR.Complaint: 3M Bair Hugger 775 Series patient warming unit reported with Error FC-050 during startup. Unit would not reliably complete startup due to stuck key/user interface fault.
CCR.Cause: Verified Error FC-050 on startup. External causes checked, including control panel contamination, physical obstruction, keypad damage, and power source. Error persisted, indicating defective user interface.
CCR.Resolution: Removed unit from service and labeled Out of Service. Replaced user interface, performed calibration, and completed operational verification. Unit returned to service after FC-050 cleared and keypad function passed.

title: 3M Bair Hugger 775 Series Patient Warming System - Error: FC-053 EEPROM Checksum Failure
description: Troubleshooting Error FC-053 on the 3M Bair Hugger 775 Series when an EEPROM checksum failure on copy #1 may prevent normal startup, affect stored operating data, or require reset, calibration, and further evaluation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the Bair Hugger 775 from patient use if Error FC-053 appears. Stop using the warmer on the patient, move warming therapy to another device if needed, and do not rely on the unit until the error is cleared and proper operation is verified.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power the unit on in a safe, non-patient-care setting and confirm the displayed error is FC-053. Note whether the error appears at startup, after standby, during temperature selection, or intermittently.

steps.title: Check External and Simple Causes First
steps.instructions: Confirm the power cord is fully seated, test from a known-good hospital-grade outlet, inspect the cord for damage, avoid questionable power sources, and check for moisture, contamination, or visible physical damage.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Unit
steps.instructions: Disconnect the unit from AC power, wait briefly, reconnect it to a known-good outlet, and observe whether Error FC-053 returns.

steps.title: Reset the Microprocessor
steps.instructions: Disconnect power. Press and hold the Bair Hugger logo button and Standby button at the same time while reconnecting the unit to power. Release the buttons when the Standby light remains on.

steps.title: Calibrate the Unit Before Return to Service
steps.instructions: Perform required operating temperature calibration, verify normal temperature selection, confirm stable airflow, and confirm Error FC-053 does not return during startup and operation.

steps.title: Stop When Resolved
steps.instructions: If the reset clears Error FC-053 and calibration/function testing passes, document the reset, calibration, and final verified status before returning the unit to service.

CCR.Complaint: 3M Bair Hugger 775 Series displayed Error FC-053, EEPROM checksum failure on copy #1. Unit was removed from patient use due to system memory fault indication.
CCR.Cause: Error was related to EEPROM checksum failure. External power connection and physical condition were checked. Fault cleared after microprocessor reset.
CCR.Resolution: Performed microprocessor reset by holding the Bair Hugger logo and Standby button while reconnecting AC power. Released buttons when Standby light remained on. Completed calibration and functional verification. Error FC-053 did not return. Unit returned to service.

title: 3M Bair Hugger 775 Series Patient Warming System - Error: FC-054 EEPROM Compare Failure
description: Troubleshooting Error FC-054 on the 3M Bair Hugger 775 Series Patient Warming System caused by an EEPROM compare failure between copy #1 and copy #2, which may require microprocessor reset, calibration, functional testing, or repair.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the warming unit from patient use if Error FC-054 is active, recurring, or if the unit cannot be trusted to operate normally. Transfer patient warming to another verified warming device if needed before troubleshooting.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the displayed error is FC-054. Determine whether it appears at startup, after power interruption, during operation, or intermittently. Ask staff whether the unit failed to heat, alarmed, shut down, or would not enter normal operation.

steps.title: Check External Power First
steps.instructions: Plug the unit into a known-good hospital-grade outlet. Inspect the power cord and plug for damage, looseness, overheating, or signs of strain. Avoid extension cords or questionable power strips during testing.

steps.title: Inspect the Unit for Obvious Physical Issues
steps.instructions: Check the housing, control panel, hose connection, and power entry area for fluid intrusion, impact damage, contamination, heat damage, or unusual odor. Verify the Bair Hugger logo button and Standby button operate normally.

steps.title: Perform the Microprocessor Reset
steps.instructions: Disconnect AC power. Press and hold the Bair Hugger logo button and Standby button at the same time. While holding both buttons, connect the unit to power. Continue holding until the Standby light remains on, then release the buttons.

steps.title: Recheck for Error FC-054
steps.instructions: Power cycle the unit normally after reset. Allow startup to complete and confirm whether FC-054 clears or returns. Watch for abnormal sounds, failure to heat, shutdown, control issues, or repeated error behavior.

steps.title: Calibrate the Unit Before Return to Service
steps.instructions: If the reset clears the error, complete operating temperature calibration and post-service functional checks per facility procedure and manufacturer service guidance. Verify airflow, heater operation, controls, and indicators.

steps.title: Return to Service Only if Fully Resolved
steps.instructions: Return the unit to service only if FC-054 clears, calibration passes, and functional testing is acceptable. Remove the Out of Service tag, clean the unit as needed, and document final device status.

CCR.Complaint: 3M Bair Hugger 775 Series reported Error FC-054. Error indicates EEPROM compare failure copy #1 to copy #2. Unit removed from patient use for Clinical Engineering evaluation.
CCR.Cause: Confirmed FC-054 memory compare error during startup. External power and physical condition checked. Error related to stored EEPROM data mismatch. Microprocessor reset required.
CCR.Resolution: Performed microprocessor reset by holding Bair Hugger logo and Standby button while connecting AC power, then released buttons once Standby light remained on. Error cleared. Completed calibration and functional testing. Unit passed checks and was returned to service.

title: 3M Bair Hugger 775 Series Patient Warming System - Error: FC-100 5 V Power Supply High / Control Board Fault
description: Troubleshooting Error FC-100 on the 3M Bair Hugger 775 Series when the 5 V power supply is above 5.333 V and the fault indicates a likely defective control board.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the warming unit from patient use if Error FC-100 is active or recurring. Stop use if connected to a patient, switch to another warming unit if active warming is required, and label the affected unit for Clinical Engineering evaluation.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power the unit on in a safe bench area, confirm Error FC-100 is displayed, note when the error appears, and check whether blower operation, heat selection, or temperature control are affected.

steps.title: Check Power Source First
steps.instructions: Plug the unit directly into a known-good hospital-grade outlet, avoid power strips or extension cords, confirm stable power, and inspect the power cord for damage.

steps.title: Inspect the Unit Externally
steps.instructions: Inspect the housing, air inlet, filter area, hose, and external condition for damage, blockage, liquid intrusion, overheating, burning smell, or loose internal debris.

steps.title: Power Cycle Once in a Controlled Bench Setting
steps.instructions: Turn the unit off, disconnect AC power, wait briefly, reconnect to a known-good outlet, and observe whether Error FC-100 returns.

steps.title: Determine Whether the Error Is Persistent
steps.instructions: If FC-100 clears, continue functional checks according to department procedure. If FC-100 returns, stop repeated startup attempts and document when the error appears.

steps.title: Isolate the Likely Failure
steps.instructions: After external power, cord condition, and visible damage are ruled out, treat recurring FC-100 as an internal power/control fault indicating a likely defective control board.

steps.title: Corrective Action After Repair
steps.instructions: Replace the control board according to approved service procedure, perform required calibration, complete functional testing, verify normal startup without FC-100, and perform required electrical safety testing after internal repair.

steps.title: Stop When Resolved
steps.instructions: After control board replacement, calibration, and post-repair testing pass, document the repair, remove the Out of Service label, and return the unit to service.

CCR.Complaint: Bair Hugger 775 removed from service for Error FC-100. Unit reports 5 V power supply voltage in excess of 5.333 V and is not cleared for patient warming use.
CCR.Cause: External power source, power cord, and visible external condition checked. Error FC-100 persisted during bench startup. Fault indicates likely defective control board.
CCR.Resolution: Removed unit from service. Replaced control board per service procedure, completed calibration, verified normal startup and operation, and returned unit to service after post-repair testing passed.

title: 3M Bair Hugger 775 Series Patient Warming System - Error: FC-101 5 V POWER SUPPLY LOW
description: Troubleshooting Error FC-101 on the 3M Bair Hugger 775 Series caused by a low 5 V power supply under 4.667 V, indicating a likely defective control board.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the warming system from patient use if Error FC-101 is active. Provide an alternate warming device if patient warming is still needed. Do not continue using the unit with an active power supply error.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power the unit on using a known-good AC outlet. Confirm Error FC-101 is displayed and note whether it appears at startup, during warmup, during active operation, or intermittently.

steps.title: Check the Power Source First
steps.instructions: Confirm the power cord is fully seated and plug the unit into a known-good hospital-grade outlet. Avoid extension cords, damaged power strips, or questionable outlets.

steps.title: Inspect the Power Cord and External Condition
steps.instructions: Inspect the power cord, appliance inlet, housing, vents, and visible external condition for damage, contamination, overheating, blocked airflow, or fluid intrusion.

steps.title: Power Cycle and Recheck the Error
steps.instructions: Turn the unit off, disconnect AC power briefly, reconnect to a known-good outlet, power the unit back on, and confirm whether Error FC-101 returns.

steps.title: Confirm the Fault Is Not Environmental or Accessory-Related
steps.instructions: Confirm the unit is not exposed to fluid, excessive heat, blocked airflow, contamination, or questionable accessory setup. Power the unit with only the required basic setup for testing.

steps.title: Identify the Likely Failure
steps.instructions: FC-101 means the system detected the 5 V power supply below 4.667 V. Treat the failure as an internal control board or power regulation issue.

steps.title: Escalate to Repair
steps.instructions: Label the unit Out of Service. Do not return it to clinical use. Replace the control board according to approved service procedures, then perform calibration and safety/functional verification.

steps.title: Stop When Resolved
steps.instructions: After repair, perform calibration, complete operational testing, verify temperature operation, confirm no active fault codes remain, and return the unit to service only after all checks pass.

CCR.Complaint: 3M Bair Hugger 775 Series removed from service for Error FC-101. Unit reported 5 V power supply under 4.667 V and was not cleared for patient use.
CCR.Cause: Verified FC-101 error during power-on testing. External power source, power cord, appliance inlet, and visible external condition checked. Error persisted, indicating likely defective control board per fault description.
CCR.Resolution: Removed unit from service and labeled Out of Service. Control board replacement required. Unit must be calibrated and pass operational/safety verification before return to service.

title: 3M Bair Hugger 775 Series Patient Warming System - Error: FC-070 Temperature out of range high.
description: Troubleshooting Error FC-070 when the 3M Bair Hugger 775 Series detects a high-temperature condition and may not be safe for patient warming.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the warming unit from patient use immediately if Error FC-070 is active or overheating is suspected. Stop using the unit for active patient warming, provide an alternate warming method if needed, and label the unit for Clinical Engineering evaluation.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the exact error displayed is Error FC-070 Temperature out of range high. Note when the error appears and check for excessive heat, weak airflow, burning smell, hose restriction, abnormal fan noise, shutdown behavior, or repeated alarming.

steps.title: Disconnect Power and Allow the Unit to Cool
steps.instructions: Turn the unit off, disconnect it from AC power, and allow it to cool completely before retesting. Inspect for obvious overheating signs, odor, damaged plastic, or abnormal physical condition.

steps.title: Check the Hose and Airflow Path
steps.instructions: Inspect the hose for kinks, blockage, collapse, cracking, contamination, or improper connection. Confirm the hose is properly seated and replace it if damaged or questionable.

steps.title: Check the Filter and External Air Intake
steps.instructions: Inspect the air filter area for dust, lint, blockage, or missing filter components. Clean or replace the filter according to site policy and manufacturer guidance, and make sure the intake is not obstructed.

steps.title: Check the Environment and Setup
steps.instructions: Confirm the unit was not covered by blankets, drapes, linen, carts, or other items that could trap heat. Check whether the hose routing or high-temperature operation contributed to heat buildup.

steps.title: Retest the Unit in a Safe Bench Setting
steps.instructions: After cooling and external checks, connect the unit to a known-good outlet and retest in a controlled non-patient-use setup. Observe airflow, temperature behavior, odor, shutdown behavior, and whether Error FC-070 returns.

steps.title: Isolate Accessory-Related Causes
steps.instructions: Test with a known-good compatible hose if the error occurred with a specific hose or setup. Determine whether the fault follows the accessory or remains with the warming unit.

steps.title: Suspect Temperature Control or Internal Heating Fault
steps.instructions: If Error FC-070 persists after cooling, airflow checks, filter inspection, environmental checks, and accessory checks, remove the unit from service for repair or formal bench evaluation.

CCR.Complaint: 3M Bair Hugger 775 Series reported Error FC-070 Temperature out of range high. Unit removed from patient use due to possible overheating/high-temperature fault.
CCR.Cause: High-temperature fault confirmed/reported. External checks performed for hose restriction, blocked airflow, filter condition, environmental obstruction, and overheating after use. Persistent fault indicates likely temperature sensing, heater, airflow, blower, or control system issue.
CCR.Resolution: Removed unit from service. Disconnected power and allowed unit to cool. Inspected hose, air intake, filter area, airflow path, and physical condition. Retested in non-patient-use setting. If Error FC-070 persisted, unit was labeled Out of Service and sent for repair/bench evaluation.

title: 3M Bair Hugger 775 Series Patient Warming System - Error: FC-071 Temperature out of range low
description: Troubleshooting Error FC-071 when the Bair Hugger 775 Series detects low temperature output, cannot reach the selected warming temperature, or cannot maintain proper warming performance.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Stop using the warmer on the patient, transfer warming support to another approved warming device if needed, and do not continue patient warming with a unit showing a temperature control error.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the displayed error is FC-071, determine when it occurs, verify whether airflow is present, and check whether the air feels cool, weak, inconsistent, or fails to warm.

steps.title: Disconnect Power, Reset, and Retest
steps.instructions: Turn the unit off, disconnect power for at least 30 seconds, reconnect to a known-good hospital-grade outlet, power the unit on, and retest warming operation.

steps.title: Check Power Source and Power Cord
steps.instructions: Inspect the power cord and outlet, try another known-good outlet, and verify the unit does not flicker, restart, or shut off during operation.

steps.title: Check Hose Connection and Airflow Path
steps.instructions: Confirm the hose is fully connected, inspect for kinks, crushing, cracks, blockage, or loose fit, and verify airflow exits the hose normally.

steps.title: Check Blanket and Setup
steps.instructions: Confirm the correct compatible warming blanket is used, the hose is properly inserted, the blanket is not folded or blocked, and warm air flows through the blanket normally.

steps.title: Inspect Filter and Air Intake Area
steps.instructions: Inspect the intake and filter area for dust, lint, labels, tape, contamination, or blockage, then clean or replace the filter according to facility procedure and manufacturer guidance.

steps.title: Retest Temperature Performance
steps.instructions: Run the unit at a selected temperature setting and confirm whether it reaches and maintains warming temperature without FC-071 returning.

steps.title: Isolate the Likely Failure Area
steps.instructions: If FC-071 returns, narrow the cause to airflow restriction, heater failure, temperature sensor issue, heater control issue, or control board function based on observed behavior.

steps.title: Stop When Resolved
steps.instructions: Return the device to service only after reset, functional testing, normal warming performance, safe physical inspection, and clear documentation.

CCR.Complaint: Bair Hugger 775 reported Error FC-071 Temperature out of range low. Staff reported unit would not reach or maintain selected warming temperature during use.
CCR.Cause: Verified FC-071 temperature low error. Checked power source, power cord, hose connection, airflow path, blanket setup, intake area, and filter condition. Error persisted during retest, indicating likely internal heater, temperature sensor, or control board related fault.
CCR.Resolution: Removed unit from service and labeled Out of Service. Sent for bench evaluation/repair due to recurring FC-071 and inability to confirm reliable warming temperature performance.

helpfulDetails: Whether air was cool, warm, or inconsistent
Any unusual sounds, heat, smell, or shutdown behavior
Whether reset cleared the error temporarily
